融合:
<color=#002461>Fusing</color> is the core gameplay mechanic of <color=#FFE32E>Plants vs. Zombies: Fusion</color>. By planting certain plants on top of other plants, according to specific <color=#002461>recipes</color>, you can fuse them into a new plant. 

Basic fusions (fusions that require only two normal plants) are the first fusions you'll encounter, and each will be unlocked as soon as you unlock the two base fusion ingredients. As you progress throughout the game, be sure to look out for even more advanced recipes!
副卡: On the Choose Your Seeds screen, after selecting a Basic Plant seed packet, you can select another one of the same type known as a Carbon Copy. The cost of a Carbon Copy is double the cost of the original one. While the new packet costs more, having two copies of the same plant means you can use it twice as often which helps a lot with building your defense and fusing certain plants quicker. Note that Doom-shroom does NOT have a Carbon Copy.
铲子:
The <color=#002461>Shovel</color> can be used to dig up plants and remove them from your lawn with no cooldown.
Tip: To quickly access the shovel, you can use the <color=#77FFF8>"1" key on your keyboard</color> to instantly pick up or put down the shovel.
手套:
<size=90%>After completing level 4 of Adventure, you will unlock the <color=#002461>Gloves</color>, which can help you control many different scenarios on the lawn.
There are two types of gloves:
    • <color=#002461>The Plant Glove</color>, which can be used to move plants and have a cooldown of <color=#77FFF8>10 seconds</color>. When moving smaller plants that can be planted multiple times in the same tile (such as Puff-shrooms), carrier plants (such as Flower Pots or Lilypads), or flying plants (such as drone fusions or Coldsnap Bean), the cooldown is reduced by <color=#77FFF8>2/3rds to around 3.33 seconds</color>. 
    • <color=#002461>The Zombie Glove</color> can be used to move zombies, with a cooldown of <color=#77FFF8>30 seconds</color>. When moving a hypnotized zombie, the cooldown is reduced to <color=#77FFF8>15 seconds</color>.
The cooldowns for both gloves are calculated independently.
Tip: To quickly access the Gloves, you can use the <color=#77FFF8>"2" key on your keyboard</color> to instantly pick up or put down the Gloves. Use the <color=#77FFF8>"V" key on your keyboard</color> to switch between the two types of gloves.
锤子:
After completing level 31 of Adventure, you will unlock the <color=#002461>Mallet</color>. The Mallet deals <color=#77FFF8>1 million</color> damage to all zombies within a small area around it and has a cooldown of <color=#77FFF8>60 seconds</color>. 
Tip: To quickly access the mallet, you can use the <color=#77FFF8>"4" key on your keyboard</color> to instantly pick up or put down the mallet.
时停:
<color=#002461>Slow Mode</color>, as the name implies, is a state where the game is fully slowed down. This helps you with more precise actions, such as fusing limited-time plants (ex. Cherry Bombs, Jalapenos) or reacting to quick events. 
When Slow Mode is enabled, the game speed will now be 0.2x the original speed; when disabled, the speed returns to the speed that you had set before (on the Main/Pause Menu). 
Tip: To quickly toggle Slow Mode, you can use the <color=#77FFF8>"3" key on your keyboard</color>.
肥料:
<size=90%>After completing level 13 of Adventure, you will unlock the plant <color=#002461>Endoflame</color> and be introduced to the <color=#002461>Fertilizer</color> mechanic. In subsequent levels, every <color=#77FFF8>70 seconds</color>, an <color=#FFE32E>Endoflame</color> will appear in the top-left corner of the lawn and provide you with a bag of Fertilizer. Some special plants, such as Endoflame, can also grant bags of Fertilizer.
Fertilizer has a variety of effects when used on plants:
    • Restores HP to a plant equal to its maximum HP, for example if a plant's maximum HP is 300 it will gain 300 health from a bag of Fertilizer (this will still cap at a plant's maximum HP unless that plant specifies otherwise).
    • Upgrades specific plants to their Upgraded variants (ex. Wall-nut → Tall-nut)
    • Transforms Scorched Threepeater back into regular Threepeater.
    • Immediately ends the <color=#77FFF8>cooldown timer</color> for certain plants (ex. Potato Mine's charge timer, Chomper's digestion time, Cob Cannon's recharge timer)
<color=#8F0000>Warning:</color> Sometimes, fertilizer can interact negatively with plants (ex. using fertilizer on Hermit-shroom will end its attack timer early).
阳光:
<color=#002461>Sun</color> is the most vital resource when it comes to planting, and it's capped at <color=#77FFF8>50,000</color> by default. In most cases, planting plants <u>REQUIRES</u> Sun, so make sure you understand how to both earn and use it effectively.

Sun comes in five different values: <color=#77FFF8>5, 15, 25, 50, and 75</color>, depending on the size of the sun drop. For example, Sun-shrooms in an early stage will produce smaller sun drops, each worth 15 sun - whereas during the day, sun will fall from the sky every <color=#77FFF8>7.5 seconds</color> and are slightly larger; granting 25 sun per drop.
金钱:
<color=#002461>Money</color> is another resource you can use in levels, measured in terms of <color=#008720>coins ($)</color> and capped at <color=#77FFF8>$99999</color>. 
You can spend coins on golden Coffee Beans to upgrade <color=#c9c9c9>silver plants</color> to their <color=#ffe600>gold upgraded variants</color>, or use coins to activate the ultimate abilities of those <color=#ffe600>gold variants</color>.
Tip: Use the <color=#77FFF8>"E" key on your keyboard</color> to quickly pick up and put down the golden Coffee Bean.
You also receive <color=#77FFF8>25 extra sun every 500 coins you spend</color>.
血量和韧性:
<color=#002461>HP</color> is the current health of a plant or zombie and is affected by how much damage they take or how much healing they receive, whereas "<color=#002461>Toughness</color>" is the measure of a plant or zombie's max HP and is only affected by some special mechanics. In most cases, HP does not exceed <color=#002461>Toughness</color>.
When percentage calculations are made based on a zombie's HP or toughness, only their base HP and Type 1 armor are considered - <u>Type 2 armor is ignored.</u>
Tip: Use the <color=#77FFF8>"Q" key on your keyboard</color> to cycle between modes of viewing plant HP statistics (hidden → numerical format → HP bar), and use the <color=#77FFF8>"W" key on your keyboard</color> to show or hide zombie HP statistics <color=#77FFF8>(if "W" is already in use for another mechanic in a level, use "R" instead)</color>.
僵尸防具:
Zombie armor is divided into <color=#002461>Type 1</color> and <color=#002461>Type 2 armor</color>.
Type 1 armor is generally assigned to <color=#77FFF8>helmet/headworn</color> armor, like a cone or bucket. It takes damage before the zombie wearing it, effectively acting as extra HP.
Type 2 armor is generally assigned to <color=#77FFF8>handheld</color> armor, like a newspaper or screendoor. It takes direct damage from different projectiles before the zombie using the armor, blocks chilling effects from direct projectiles, and absorbs splash damage from ignited projectiles.
护盾:
<color=#002461>Shield</color> values can be thought of as extra health for plants. Whenever a plant takes damage, its shield is depleted first before its main HP.
When a plant has a shield value, it also has its damage reduced by <color=#77FFF8>20%</color>.
护甲系数:
<color=#002461>DR value</color>, or <color=#002461>Damage Reduction value</color> is one of the damage reduction mechanics in the game used for zombies. Each zombie possesses their own DR value, which can be affected in various ways, such as interactions with other zombies or a mode you are playing. When a zombie takes damage, the damage they take is multiplied by a factor of <color=#77FFF8>100/(100 + DR Value)</color>. As DR value increases, the damage reduction factor increases greatly; causing zombies to take less and less damage.
A few example values are: 
    • 100 DR Value = 100/(100 + 100) = 0.5 → 50% damage reduction (Zombies only take 50% of the original damage)
    • 300 DR Value = 100/(100 + 300) = 0.25 → 75% damage reduction (Zombies only take 25% of the original damage)
    • 900 DR Value = 100/(100 + 900) = 0.1 → 90% damage reduction (Zombies only take 10% of the original damage)
临界值:
Most zombies possess the <color=#002461>critical threshold</color> mechanic. When a zombie's health drops below a certain threshold, usually <color=#77FFF8>1/3 of their base HP</color>, their head will fall off and they will stop attacking. Their HP will deplete quickly over time until reaching 0 and fully dying.
Note that the head of a zombie dropping will register as a kill for plants that have a kill condition.
Some zombies do not have a critical threshold, including (but not limited to) Gargantuars, Mecha Gargantuars, and Silver/Gold statues.
低矮植物: <color=#002461>Short plants</color> are small in terms of height and therefore cannot be hit by horizontal projectiles from zombies.
高大植物: <color=#002461>Tall plants</color> are large in terms of height and can block the <color=#77FFF8>normal jumps</color> of Pole Vaulter, Pogo, and Dolphin Rider zombies.
巨型植物: <color=#002461>Titan plants</color> occupy two tiles; when performing actions on them such as fusing, moving, or shoveling, their position is determined by the leftmost tile of the two they occupy.
可密植植物: <color=#002461>Stackable plants</color> are compact in size, and can be planted up to 3 times in a single tile. When fusing one plant with a stackable plant, all stackable plants in that tile will also fuse at the same time with that plant (if possible).
坚实植物: <color=#002461>Defensive plants</color> usually have high health and will show signs of damage when dropping below 2/3 and 1/3 of their maximum HP. Ladder Zombies can only place ladders on defensive plants.
金属植物:
<color=#002461>Metal plants</color> can be fused with various metal objects found in game and can be given metal objects to restore 500 HP.
Tip: If a plant is fused with a bucket, football helmet, or Mecha-nut fragment, the damage taken from cherry explosions will be halved.
红温:
Some plants can inflict the <color=#8F0000>Enflamed</color> debuff.
Zombies that are affected with the enflamed debuff take <color=#77FFF8>50%</color> more damage and explode on death; dealing <color=#77FFF8>100</color> damage to zombies in a 1-tile radius and spreading the enflamed debuff to those same zombies (can also affect aerial zombies).
The enflamed debuff lasts <color=#77FFF8>indefinitely</color>, but will be removed and cannot stack (unless otherwise specified) with chill/freeze/poison debuffs.
寒冷与冻结:
Some plants can inflict the <color=#008AE6>chill</color>/<color=#008AE6>freeze</color> debuff, or add <color=#3e599c>cryo points</color> to zombies.
    • <color=#008AE6>Chilled</color>: Zombies that are chilled are slowed down by <color=#77FFF8>50%</color>. 
    • <color=#0099ff>Frozen</color>: Zombies that are frozen stop moving entirely, chilled for 10 seconds, and have the timer of their chill status paused until they are no longer frozen.
    • <color=#3e599c>Cryo Points</color>: When a zombie that is not currently frozen reachs a certain <color=#77FFF8>threshold</color> of cryo points, they are frozen for 4 seconds and their threshold will increase by 100 points. When they become unfrozen, their cryo points are reset to 0. The cryo point threshold for freezing starts at <color=#77FFF8>100</color> and is capped at <color=#77FFF8>1000</color> cryo points.
Cryo points are only reset when a zombie unfreezes.
Both the chill and freeze debuffs are immediately removed by the <color=#8F0000>Enflamed</color> debuff or ignited projectiles.
蒜毒:
Some plants can add <color=#5E1675>poison points</color> to zombies. 
The normal <color=#5E1675>poison point cap</color> is 5 points. 
When zombies are inflicted with Poison points, but they already have 5 poison points, they will be <color=#77FFF8>poisoned</color> for 5 seconds.
<color=#5E1675>Poisoned Status</color>: A zombie will take (20 × Poison Points) damage every second they are poisoned.
<color=#5E1675>Toxic Effect</color>: Inflicts a 10-second Poison status on a zombie. If a zombie already has <color=#77FFF8>5 poison points</color>, their poison point cap will increase to <color=#77FFF8>30</color>, and while this status is active all other applications of poison points will trigger the effect again.
Note: Poison effects are removed by the <color=#8F0000>Enflamed</color> debuff.
光照等级:
Some plants can either provide or utilize <color=#4C3D06>Lumos levels</color>, usually linked to <color=#77FFF8>Plantern-infused plants</color>. Lumos levels can strengthen or buff certain plants and their unique mechanics.
Note: There is no limit or cap on how many Lumos levels a single tile can have.
水草值:
Certain plants, usually aquatic or Tangle Kelp related, can apply <color=#025e1b>Tangle points</color> to zombies in water. If tangle points are applied:
    • If a zombie already has 6 tangle points, the attack will deal <color=#77FFF8>1% of the zombie's HP</color> as extra damage.
    • If a zombie already has 30 tangle points, the attack will <color=#77FFF8>instantly kill them</color>.
Furthermore, if a zombie with tangle points is killed (by certain Tangle plants), a <color=#77FFF8>Tingle Kelp</color> will spawn on the tile where the zombie died if possible. 
Certain Tangle plants can also apply <color=#025e1b>tangle points</color> to zombies on land, however the extra damage from land tangle points is <color=#77FFF8>1/3</color> of the previously mentioned effects. 
Attacks that apply any kind of tangle points also inflict a 2-second <color=#025e1b>"Tangled"</color> debuff, which slows zombies down by 50% and can be stacked with other slowing effects like chill.
磁力系统:
Most magnet-infused plants (referred to as magnetic plants) can link to other plants in the 8 surrounding tiles with something called a <color=#666699>Magnetic System</color>.
Each magnetic plant in a Magnetic System count as 1 <color=#666699>magnetic link</color> to other plants in that same system.
Some magnetic plants gain enhanced abilities or buffs depending on the amount of magnetic links they have.
Note: There is no limit or cap to how many magnetic links can be in one system or counted by the plants in that system.
传送状态:
Zombies that are put in the <color=#00008B>chronoshift state</color> will completely stop moving, and the amount of time that passes while they are stuck in place is saved as a value (referred to as <color=#77FFF8>"Elapsed Time"</color> in most entries.)
If (at any point in that they are in the chronoshift state) a zombie's HP is less than <color=#77FFF8>(30 * Elapsed Time)</color>, they will be instantly killed; otherwise, they will take <color=#77FFF8>(30 * Elapsed Time)</color> damage when they exit the chronoshift state.
余烬状态:
Certain doom-infused plants can inflict the <color=#460075>irradiated</color> debuff on zombies. When the irradiated status is combined with other debuffs/status effects, it can enhance them.
    • <color=#008AE6>Chilled</color>: Zombies are slowed down by an extra <color=#77FFF8>10% (50% → 60%)</color>
    • <color=#8F0000>Enflamed</color>: Damage boost increases by <color=#77FFF8>10% (50% → 60%)</color>
    • <color=#069923>Poison</color>: The Poison duration becomes <color=#77FFF8>infinite</color>.
冻结: <color=#002461>Frozen plants</color>, as the name implies, are completely immobilized. Fire-type plants, Ice-type plants, Snow plants, flying/carrier plants, and plants protected by <color=#77FFF8>Firnace and its fusions</color> are immune to freezing.
暴风雪:
<color=#002461>Blizzards</color> occur during flags/huge waves on Snow maps. Blizzards will <color=#77FFF8>encase plants in iceblocks</color> (triggering effects that check for plants dying).
All plants immune to freezing along with Titan and Pumpkin plants will be immune to blizzards.
熔岩地块:
<color=#610000>Lava tiles</color> can spawn randomly on maps like the Beach map, damaging plants that are on top of it every second.
    • If a pot is on the tile, the lava will deal <color=#77FFF8>10 damage</color> to the pot first.
    • If there is no pot, the lava will deal <color=#77FFF8>50 damage</color> to the plant on top of it.
Tip: Nut Pot itself and the plant on top of it will be <color=#77FFF8>immune</color> to the damage from lava tiles.
诸神模式:
In <color=#002461>"The Gods"</color>, you can only select a few plants that you will upgrade or revive over time in the level using sun.
The Plant Gloves will have no cooldown but you cannot use the Zombie Gloves or Shovel, and you <color=#8F0000><u>CANNOT</u></color> fuse plants in this mode.
Killing zombies will reward sun, proportional to the zombie's HP.
Note: You can adjust the playing mode of "The Gods" levels in the main settings menu, switching between having to manually pick up the gloves to move plants or automatically picking up the plants by clicking on them (this may interfere with plants that have on-click mechanics).
旅行生存模式:
In <color=#002461>"Odyssey: Survival"</color>, you must build a defense and survive <color=#77FFF8>21 rounds over 7 different maps (3 rounds per map)</color>.
After each round, zombies gain a <color=#77FFF8>20% HP boost (further multiplied by 1.5 on difficulty 5)</color>.
After winning a round, you will be given the option to obtain a modifier for your plants/defense. Skipping the modifier selection will grant you <color=#77FFF8>1,000 points</color>, which can be used in the Odyssey Shop.
After completing <color=#77FFF8>round 5</color>, you will be presented with the Odyssey shop rather than the modifier selection. This will also occur every <color=#77FFF8>3 rounds afterwards (5, 8, 11, etc.)</color>
Note: Fusion recipes for Epic Odyssey plants and stronger modifiers, such as Pets and Synergies, can only be found in the shop.
旅行：炼狱难度:
In <color=#002461>Purgatory difficulty</color>, after selecting a plant modifier, you must also select a zombie modifier, which can strengthen the zombies or harm your defense.
Similarly, after exiting the Odyssey Shop, you will have to select a <color=#310052><u>mini-boss</u></color> that will now appear in the next round and every 3 rounds afterwards <color=#77FFF8>(example: If you select a mini-boss on round 5, it will appear in rounds 6, 9, 12, and so on)</color>.
旅行：诅咒难度:
<size=90%>In <color=#002461>Cursed difficulty</color>, you can select up to <color=#77FFF8>2 plant modifiers</color> BUT you will have to select the <color=#77FFF8>corresponding amount</color> of zombie modifiers afterwards. Similar to Purgatory difficulty, you will still have to select a <color=#310052><u>mini-boss</u></color> after leaving the Odyssey Shop.
<color=#c20000><u>Four irrevocable curses befall the curse-bearer:</u></color>
    • You cannot purchase modifiers that cost more than <color=#77FFF8>15,000 points</color>.
    • You cannot use Avarice-shroom or Kelptain Goldbeard.
    • Plants take <color=#77FFF8>2x</color> the normal amount of damage, and their healing is reduced by <color=#77FFF8>30%</color>.
    • Zombies take <color=#77FFF8>50%</color> less damage and have <color=#77FFF8>2x</color> their normal amount of HP.
<color=#2aff00><u>Yet to the tenacious, four blessings shall be revealed:</u></color>
    • You will receive <color=#77FFF8>2x</color> the normal amount of points.
    • Any sun gain will be increased by <color=#77FFF8>300%</color>.
    • You will receive <color=#77FFF8>2 extra rerolls</color> each round.
    • You can find unique modifiers in the Odyssey Shop.
胆小菇之梦:
<size=90%>In <color=#002461>"Scaredy's Dream"</color>, use the <color=#77FFF8>Scaredy-shroom</color> you are provided at the start of the level that <color=#77FFF8>continuously shoots</color> to trigger other plants that shoot projectiles <color=#77FFF8>(by having the Scaredy spores hit them)</color>.
Pea-shooting plants have been altered in the mode to take advantage of this mechanic, specifically Repeater and Gatling pea will now fire <color=#77FFF8>one pea forwards, one downwards, and one upwards</color>.
